result.summary: The page is mostly visually coherent, but several elements near the bottom are hard to read or appear obstructed. The most noticeable problems are the scroll prompt covering legal text, extremely small disclaimer copy, and some crowded fixed navigation/header areas.

issues.description: The centered “SCROLL DOWN” overlay sits directly on top of the legal/disclaimer text, making that content partially unreadable.
issues.suggestion: Move the scroll prompt higher, reduce its opacity only if it does not obscure text, or hide it before the disclaimer section becomes visible.

issues.description: The legal/disclaimer copy at the bottom is extremely small and has poor readability against the yellow background, especially with other overlays nearby.
issues.suggestion: Increase the font size, improve line spacing, and ensure sufficient color contrast for required legal text.

issues.description: The bottom navigation appears fixed and visually crowded against the page content, leaving very little breathing room above it.
issues.suggestion: Add more bottom padding to the page content so important text and controls are not crowded by the sticky navigation bar.

issues.description: The top header contains several logos, a hamburger icon, state label, and a large Join Now button in a tight space, making the header feel cramped on mobile.
issues.suggestion: Simplify the header on mobile by reducing logo count/size, moving secondary brand marks into a menu, or giving the Join Now button slightly less width.
